stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released April 29, 2026: net profit Rs 1,259.10 cr (+22.22% YoY, +20.93% QoQ) — highest-ever quarterly PAT. NII Rs 3,172.61 cr (+19.6% QoQ), highest in bank's history. Reported NIM expanded 56 bps QoQ to 3.74% (3.20% ex-one-offs, +2 bps QoQ / +8 bps YoY). FY26 annual PAT Rs 4,345 cr — best ever. Asset quality at decadal lows: GNPA 1.62%, NNPA 0.37%, credit cost 47 bps. CASA crossed Rs 1 lakh cr for first time at Rs 1,03,390 cr (+8.26% QoQ, ~+21% YoY); NR deposits also above Rs 1 lakh cr. Fee income record Rs 990.92 cr. Final dividend Rs 1.20/share (60% of face value) recommended. April 30, 2026: announced acquisition of part of Standard Chartered Bank India credit cards portfolio — incremental fee/retail growth lever. Stock around Rs 286-288 (April 30 close Rs 286.80). MD & CEO KVS Manian guided FY27 priorities: gold loans, LAP, commercial banking, cards, MFI; ~100 branch additions planned.
stocks.sector_context: Indian private banking sector constructive. RBI repo at 5.25% (held in Feb 2026 MPC after earlier cuts) — supports NIM stabilization with residual deposit-repricing tailwind into Q1/early-Q2 FY27. System bank credit growth robust at ~16-17% in FY26, outpacing deposit growth and tightening liquidity. Nifty Private Bank index outperforming Nifty PSU Bank. Mid/small private banks have seen credit growth outpace deposits in FY26 — a sector-wide profitability tailwind. Near-term overhang: West Asia geopolitical tensions flagged in govt commentary as dampening lending outlook.
stocks.peer_comparison: Federal Bank is a consensus top pick among mid-cap private banks. Emkay Global SMID top picks include Federal Bank (TP Rs 270) alongside Karur Vysya Bank (TP Rs 325) and Karnataka Bank, citing strong RoA delivery and limited unsecured retail risk. YES Securities prefers Federal Bank with KVB, BOB, SBI, Axis, Indian Bank. Sell-side consensus: 24 BUY / 7 HOLD / 1 SELL out of 33 analysts; consensus TP ~Rs 255-257, range Rs 210-325 (Motilal Oswal Rs 245). Federal Bank's GNPA 1.62% / NNPA 0.37% is competitive with KVB (~0.77% GNPA on TTM) and superior to most PSU peers. Federal Bank trades roughly in line / slight premium to mid-cap private peers on price-to-book post Q4 rally.
red_flags: Stock at ~Rs 287 has already exceeded Emkay Global TP Rs 270 and Motilal Oswal TP Rs 245 — limited near-term analyst-implied upside (~+8% to consensus average)
Q4 FY26 EPS missed estimates by -0.79% despite revenue +5.68% beat — bottom-line expectations were rich
Reported NIM 3.74% flattered by one-offs; underlying core NIM only 3.20% (+2 bps QoQ)
Sector-wide West Asia geopolitical risk flagged as dampening near-term lending outlook
Mid/small private banks running credit > deposit growth — incremental deposit competition could pressure CoF in later FY27
catalysts: Standard Chartered India credit-cards portfolio acquisition (announced Apr 30, 2026) — fee income and retail customer base accretion
FY27 NIM tailwind from residual deposit repricing in Q1 / early Q2 FY27 per management commentary
Final dividend Rs 1.20/share record date and AGM (post Q4 announcement) — pending shareholder approval
~100 new branch additions planned in FY27 — distribution scale-up
Management strategic re-acceleration in chosen segments (gold, LAP, commercial banking, cards, MFI) after FY26 balance-sheet realignment
KVS Manian articulated ambition for top-5 private sector bank ranking — re-rating optionality if execution sustains
